name: CRISPR Off-Target Predictor
description: Scans a reference DNA sequence for potential CRISPR off-target sites by sliding a window across both strands and counting mismatches against the protospacer. Sites within the mismatch threshold are scored using seed-region weighting (Hsu et al. 2013) and PAM presence, then ranked by risk level (HIGH, MEDIUM, LOW).

inputs.name: protospacer
inputs.description: The 20 bp DNA protospacer sequence from the gRNA design step (without PAM). Standard bases A, T, G, C only.

inputs.name: reference
inputs.description: Reference DNA sequence to scan for off-target sites. Accepts a resource name (e.g. 'pBR322'), a raw sequence string, a FASTA-formatted string, or a GenBank-formatted string. The framework resolves these automatically.

inputs.name: max_mismatches
inputs.description: Maximum number of mismatches allowed for a site to be reported as a potential off-target. Default is 3. Values above 4 may produce many low-confidence results.

outputs.description: A dictionary containing the protospacer, reference length, number of sites evaluated, a ranked list of off-target sites with position/strand/mismatch/risk details, and a one-sentence specificity summary.
